chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D –1251 of 2017 Constitutional Petition No. D –7433 of 2018 Date order with signature of Judge(s) CP No. D –1251 of 2017 For direction: 1. For order on MIT-II report dated 20.04.2020 2. For hearing of Misc. No.17774/2020 (Contempt) CP No. D –7433 of 2018 Direction: For hearing of Misc. No.17333/2020 (Contempt) 02.03.2022 Petitioner present in person in CP No.D –1251 of 2017 Mr. Ali Safdar Depar, AAG Mr. Altaf Hussain, advocate for KMC alongwith Toureer Ahmed Siyal KMC Mr. Sarwar Ali, advocate for KMC Korangi Mr. Mushahid Anwar, Director Education Korangi ------------------------------------ These petitions were disposed of vide order dated 11.02.2020, an excerpt whereof is as under: “These two petitions were filed on the ground that concerned Municipal Commissioners are not sending the cases of the petitioners for consideration of promotion from BS-14 to BS-16 for DPC. Counsel representing the DMC/respondents state that the matters of the petitioners have been sent to the concerned Municipal Commissioner, DMC Korangi to the Secretary Local Government for perusal and to place the same before the DPC. The Secretary Local Government is directed that without getting influenced with the said report of the DMC Korangi to consider the cases of the petitioners, sent by the concerned Municipal Commissioners with regard to their promotion from BS-14 to BS-16, and decide the same through DPC strictly in accordance with law within a period of 60 days from the date of receipt of this order. Compliance report be submitted to this Court through MIT-II.

The Secretary Local Government is directed that without getting influenced with the said report of the DMC Korangi to consider the cases of the petitioners, sent by the concerned Municipal Commissioners with regard to their promotion from BS-14 to BS-16, and decide the same through DPC strictly in accordance with law within a period of 60 days from the date of receipt of this order. Compliance report be submitted to this Court through MIT-II. With these directions the instant petitions are disposed of with the consent of the parties along with all the listed and pending application(s). Let a copy of this order be sent to the Secretary Local Government of Sindh for information and compliance.” The petitioner in CP No.D –1251 of 2017, who is present in person and submitted that since 2011 she is in Grade-14 and during the service she has qualified M.A. DTC, CT, B.Ed and M.Ed and she is only striving for her promotion in Grade-16 but despite having such high qualification she has not been considered for promotion. Per petitioner, despite recommendations by DPC, the case of the petitioner has not been considered for promotion from BS-14 to 16 along with benefits. She prayed for initiation of contempt proceedings against the alleged contemnors. Learned counsel representing DMC Korangi has submitted that the petitioner’s name appears at Sr. No.38 of the Seniority List of Junior School Teachers in BPS-16, DMC Korangi Page 2 of 2 prepared on 01.03.2022, which complies with the order dated 22.02.2022 passed by this court. He prayed for dismissal of the contempt application. We have heard the petitioner who is present in person, learned counsel representing the DMC Korangi and perused the material available on record. The Final Seniority List of JSTs of DMC Korangi shows the following position: Sr. No. Emp.No. Name F/H Name Desig BPS Date of Birth Date of Appt. Last Promotion Date Date of Retirement Qualification Zone Acade mic Professi onal B.A./B. Com/B. Sc. Grade/ Div B.Ed. Grade/D iv. 38 27409 0 Nazia Tauseef Tauseef Ahmed JST 16 29.09.1972 22.10.1992 24.02.2010 28.09.2032 1998 2002 Shah Faisl 13 E0178 0 Rubina Abbas Muham mad Abbas JST 14 06.09.1967 09.09.1989 13.02.1997 05.09.2027 1989 1995 Land hi As per learned counsel representing DMC Korangi, the petitioner was appointed as PST (BS-07) in 1992 in Municipal Committee West, later on, she was promoted to the post of JST (BS-14) which post is actually in BS-09, instead of BS-14; and she ought to have been repatriated to her parent district i.e.

Grade/ Div B.Ed. Grade/D iv. 38 27409 0 Nazia Tauseef Tauseef Ahmed JST 16 29.09.1972 22.10.1992 24.02.2010 28.09.2032 1998 2002 Shah Faisl 13 E0178 0 Rubina Abbas Muham mad Abbas JST 14 06.09.1967 09.09.1989 13.02.1997 05.09.2027 1989 1995 Land hi As per learned counsel representing DMC Korangi, the petitioner was appointed as PST (BS-07) in 1992 in Municipal Committee West, later on, she was promoted to the post of JST (BS-14) which post is actually in BS-09, instead of BS-14; and she ought to have been repatriated to her parent district i.e. District West rather than claiming seniority and other service benefits from DMC West. In support of his contentions, he relied upon the letter dated 18.02.2019 and the statement dated 07.08.2019. At this stage, we confronted him that vide minutes of the meeting of Departmental Promotion Committee DMC Korangi on 13.01.2021, the petitioner was recommended from BS-14 to BS-16, Education Department, DMC Korangi, however, learned counsel insisted that as per Final Seniority List dated 01.03.2022, the petitioner has been placed in BS-16, therefore, the compliance of the order has been made. From the above facts and circumstances of the case, prima facie, the compliance of the order passed by this court has been substantially made, therefore, we are not inclined to stretch the matter furthermore on the listed applications, leaving the petitioner at liberty to assail the Seniority List, if her cause still subsists, before the proper forum under law. Both the listed applications are dismissed. J U D G E J U D G E Nadir*
